diagrams.title: AI Risk Classification Pyramid
diagrams.description: Visual representation of EU AI Act risk categories from minimal to unacceptable risk
elements: MINIMAL RISK (bottom): Recommendation systems, spam filters, video games
LIMITED RISK (middle): Chatbots, AI-generated content labeling
HIGH RISK (upper): Hiring AI, credit scoring, facial recognition, medical devices
UNACCEPTABLE RISK (peak): Social scoring, real-time biometric identification in public spaces
diagrams.purpose: Understanding regulatory requirements for different AI applications and why certain AI uses are restricted or banned.

diagrams.title: AI Decision-Making Accountability Chain
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing who is accountable at each stage of AI system lifecycle
elements: STAGE 1: Data Collection - Data Engineers accountable
STAGE 2: Model Training - ML Engineers accountable
STAGE 3: Model Testing - QA Team accountable
STAGE 4: Deployment Decision - AI Ethics Committee accountable
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying organizational accountability structures for AI outcomes, ensuring clear responsibility at each development stage.
